element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Three standard gadgets per character plus two custom selections from available list
Four secret gadgets discoverable during gameplay
Enemies learnable in training mode as playable opponents
Two co-op modes: collaborative (shared credits, no friendly fire) or competitive (separate credits, mutual damage allowed)
Pressure mechanic creating frustration per reviewer feedback
Significant in SNES/Genesis/PC versions; minimized in Game Boy/Game Gear versions

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sluggish gameplay with delayed combat feedback; punch/kick inputs continue registering well after enemy defeat, creating rhythm confusion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden blueprint gadgets discoverable in levels

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat sequences with basic enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Puzzle integration increases; gadget selection strategy becomes important
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 3 introduces time limits and escalating frustration
notable_spikes: Lack of continues system creates cumulative difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level begins with player choosing three standard gadgets plus two custom selections, creating tactical variety and replay incentive
SNES/Genesis/PC versions integrate puzzle-solving between combat encounters; Game Boy/Game Gear versions strip puzzles for streamlined platform fighting
Two distinct cooperative modes available: collaborative (shared economy) or competitive (individual scoring with mutual damage)

sources_used: Wikipedia: Batman Forever (video game) - Gameplay section
Wikipedia: Batman Forever (video game) - Reception section
